original_hypothesis.title: Genesis deposits can retroactively earn past Gauge rewards due to updateReward(address(0)) in depositsForGenesis
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: GaugeV2.depositsForGenesis calls _depositsForGenesis which uses updateReward(address(0)) rather than updateReward(tokenOwner). This leaves userRewardPerTokenPaid[tokenOwner] at 0 while their balance becomes nonzero via genesisPool.balanceOf(tokenOwner) inclusion. When the user later calls getReward(), earned() uses (rewardPerToken() - userRewardPerTokenPaid[user]) and can include historical rewardPerTokenStored, letting the genesis depositor claim rewards accumulated before their deposit—effectively stealing from existing stakers.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is directly stated by Phase A invariant INV-STATE-001 with a clear economic theft path. It is low realism for external attackers because it requires genesisPool calling depositsForGenesis (privileged), but it is a critical operational defect to validate and remediate.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _depositsForGenesis(... ) internal updateReward(address(0)) { ... maturityTime[_tokenOwner] = _timestamp; } // does not checkpoint userRewardPerTokenPaid[_tokenOwner]

preconditions: genesisPool is set and calls depositsForGenesis for a user
Gauge has nonzero rewardPerTokenStored from prior distributions (notifyRewardAmount called before genesis deposit or before user's first reward checkpoint)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Genesis deposit updates global reward state but not user checkpoint; _balanceOf includes genesisPool.balanceOf(user)
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Requires genesisPool activity; could also be triggered by misordered operations
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time rewards have accumulated prior to genesis deposits

operational_questions: Can notifyRewardAmount occur before genesis deposits in real lifecycle?
Does genesisPool.balanceOf immediately reflect depositsForGenesis amounts at the time users claim?
How large can retroactive claims be relative to gauge reward balance?
validation_requirements: Reproduce on testnet: distribute rewards, then deposit via genesis, then claim
Quantify theft from existing stakers across multiple users
Review genesis launch sequencing scripts and operational runbooks

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Ensure gauge has accumulated rewardPerTokenStored via prior reward distributions
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: GenesisPool deposits LP on behalf of attacker-controlled user via depositsForGenesis
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: User immediately calls getReward() to claim retroactive rewards
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Repeat with multiple accounts if possible

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Reward pool drained unfairly; legitimate stakers lose emissions

execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Depends on whether rewards can start before genesis deposits finish

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Large first-time reward claims immediately after genesis deposits for accounts with prior userRewardPerTokenPaid=0
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Unexpected reward depletion during genesis phase
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Many new accounts claiming unusually high rewards without corresponding staking history

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
